Wade buys three sandwiches and two drinks that cost four dollars each. The total spent is twenty-six dollars which means the drinks cost eight dollars total. The remaining money for the sandwiches is eighteen dollars so each sandwich costs six dollars. The lab [address] [phone] [email] [name] confirms the price is correct for every item.

The calculation shows that six dollars is the cost per sandwich. Wade can now plan his trip with the food he bought. He has enough to eat now, on the road, and in the evening.
